content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates strong AoA methodology fidelity throughout. Turn 2 invokes a state-the-want move ('what's it like to say that out loud'). Turn 4 is a clean find-the-want-behind-the-should question ('what would a week in the Cascades actually give you?'), letting the want be felt concretely. Turn 6 names the linguistic pattern directly ('put it on trial...prosecute it') — a precise read of the linguistic-caveat. Turn 8 refuses the verdict explicitly and reframes ('the question isn't really about the week...where did you learn that wanting needed a trial?') — this hits childhood-imprinting at the right moment. Turn 10 reflects the client's self-abandonment ('you answered your own question and then handed it back'). Turn 12 stays with the feeling rather than jumping to compassion or solution. The coach avoids all listed traps: no verdict issued, no spiritual bypass to 'deep wants are generous,' no premature partner-empathy. Methodology serves the moment rather than decorating it.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "State-the-want move — invites client to feel saying the want aloud rather than debating it."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Find-the-want-behind-the-should — concrete, sensory inquiry into what the week would give."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the linguistic-caveat pattern ('put it on trial / prosecute') precisely when it's live."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses to issue a verdict — names why the verdict won't do what client hopes; pivots to childhood-imprinting question."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the self-abandonment move (handing the question back) — keeps the locus with the client."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the feeling ('what's it like to see that right now?') — does not jump to compassion or fix."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "notes": "The signature question 'what if you acted exactly how you want' wasn't deployed verbatim, but functionally equivalent inquiries (turns 2, 4) served the same purpose. Anti-patterns spiritual-narcissism and dont-jump-to-compassion-before-anger were both successfully avoided — coach let anger surface in turn 13 organically."
}
